# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.214.0/http/server.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  username: string;
  email: string;
  status: "active" | "inactive" | "pending";
  createdAt: string;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

function generateId(): string {
  return crypto.randomUUID();
}

async function handlePostUser(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const user: Omit<User, "id" | "createdAt"> = await request.json();

    if (!user.username || !user.email || !user.status) {
      return new Response("Missing required fields", { status: 400 });
    }
    if (!["active", "inactive", "pending"].includes(user.status)) {
        return new Response("Invalid status. Must be 'active', 'inactive', or 'pending'.", { status: 400 });
    }

    const userId = generateId();
    const newUser: User = {
      id: userId,
      username: user.username,
      email: user.email,
      status: user.status,
      createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    };

    const usernameLower = user.username.toLowerCase();
    const statusLower = user.status.toLowerCase();
    const emailLower = user.email.toLowerCase();

    const ok = await kv.atomic()
      .set(["users", userId], newUser)
      .set(["users_by_username", usernameLower, userId], true)
      .set(["users_by_email", emailLower, userId], true)
      .set(["users_by_status", statusLower, userId], true)
      .commit();

    if (!ok.ok) {
      return new Response("Failed to add user", { status: 500 });
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(newUser), {
      status: 201,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error adding user:", error);
    return new Response(`Error: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function handleGetUsers(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    const params = url.searchParams;

    const filters: { field: string; value: string }[] = [];
    if (params.has("username")) {
      filters.push({ field: "username", value: params.get("username")!.toLowerCase() });
    }
    if (params.has("email")) {
      filters.push({ field: "email", value: params.get("email")!.toLowerCase() });
    }
    if (params.has("status")) {
      filters.push({ field: "status", value: params.get("status")!.toLowerCase() });
    }

    let userIds: Set<string> | null = null;

    if (filters.length === 0) {
      const users: User[] = [];
      const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });
      for await (const entry of iter) {
        users.push(entry.value);
      }
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(users), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    for (const filter of filters) {
      const currentFilterUserIds = new Set<string>();
      const indexPrefix = [`users_by_${filter.field}`, filter.value];
      const iter = kv.list({ prefix: indexPrefix });

      for await (const entry of iter) {
        const userId = entry.key[entry.key.length - 1] as string;
        currentFilterUserIds.add(userId);
      }

      if (userIds === null) {
        userIds = currentFilterUserIds;
      } else {
        userIds = new Set([...userIds].filter(id => currentFilterUserIds.has(id)));
      }

      if (userIds.size === 0) {
        return new Response(JSON.stringify([]), {
          headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        });
      }
    }

    if (userIds === null || userIds.size === 0) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify([]), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    const keysToGet = [...userIds].map(id => ["users", id]);
    const entries = await kv.getMany<User>(keysToGet);

    const users: User[] = [];
    for (const entry of entries) {
      if (entry.value) {
        users.push(entry.value);
      }
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(users), {
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error searching users:", error);
    return new Response(`Error: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);

  if (url.pathname === "/users") {
    if (request.method === "POST") {
      return await handlePostUser(request);
    } else if (request.method === "GET") {
      return await handleGetUsers(request);
    }
  }

  return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
}

serve(handler);
